data.title: REG - Convatec Group PLC - Director/PDMR Shareholding
data.description:
RNS Number : 0930Y Convatec Group PLC 04 September 2025  

4 September 2025

Convatec Group Plc

("Convatec" or "the Company")

Director/PDMR Shareholding

Exercise of options - "Save As You Earn" share option scheme

The Company has been notified of the following transactions in relation to Persons Discharging Managerial Responsibility ("PDMR") in respect of ordinary shares of 10 pence each in the Company ("Shares").

Following the appointment of Fiona Ryder on 4 August 2025 as Interim Chief Financial Officer and PDMR, we have been notified that on 2 September 2025, Ms. Ryder exercised options over 10,346 Shares. The options were granted on 14 July 2022 under the Convatec 2017 Save as You Earn scheme, at an option price of 173.97 pence per Share.

About Convatec

Pioneering trusted medical solutions to improve the lives we touch: Convatec is a global medical products and technologies company, focused on solutions for the management of chronic conditions, with leading positions in Advanced Wound Care, Ostomy Care, Continence Care, and Infusion Care. With more than 10,000 colleagues, we provide our products and services in around 90 countries, united by a promise to be forever caring. Our solutions provide a range of benefits, from infection prevention and protection of at-risk skin, to improved patient outcomes and reduced care costs. Convatec's revenues in 2024 were over $2 billion. The company is a constituent of the FTSE 100 Index . To learn more please visit http://www.convatecgroup.com

data.title: Lumber Prices Buoyed by Big Sawmill Curtailment — WSJ
data.description:
By Ryan Dezember

Lumber prices that have dropped more than 20% over the past month are prompting one of North America's largest producers to throttle back output by 12%.

Interfor said Thursday that it would reduce hours and reconfigure shifts as well as lengthen holiday breaks and maintenance shutdowns at its mills in Canada and the U.S. to reduce output by about 145 million board feet through year-end. Lumber futures, which had fallen 18 of the past 22 trading sessions since hitting a three-year high Aug. 1, rose in response.

Lumber prices ran up this summer ahead of a big rise in the antidumping and countervailing duties levied on imports from Canada. Those duties-now about 35% for most Canadian sawmills, up from about 15%,-are part of a long-running trade dispute and are separate from tariffs that President Trump has threatened to slap on imported wood in the name of national security.

Interfor, which has headquarters in British Columbia, is among the big Canadian sawyers that have shifted operations into the U.S. as duties and diminished log availability have put sawmills out of the money back home.

About 50% of Interfor's capacity these days is in the U.S. South, where a glut of pine trees makes for the highest sawmill margins. Another 12% is Washington and Oregon, where mills compete fiercely with Canadian rivals to sell the same species of wood.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: ICE Canola Midday: Looking to Turn Positive
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--Canola futures on the Intercontinental Exchange were attempting to shed their losses Thursday morning, despite little support from comparable oils.

An analyst said canola has been dropping back this week due to increased yield expectations for this year and China's tariffs on Canadian canola seed and its products.

"The market is trying to find a level that incorporates these things," the analyst said.

He added that harvest pressure is building and there's softness in Chicago soybeans.

Not only were the soybeans lower, but the soyoil as well, while the soymeal was narrowly mixed. Declines in European rapeseed and Malaysian palm added more pressure on canola, while modest losses in crude oil weighed on the vegetable oils.

Manitoba reported its overall harvest was 29% complete, with its canola at 10% finished.

The Canadian dollar was falling back by mid-session Thursday, with the loonie at 72.24 U.S. cents compared with Wednesday's close of 72.50.

Approximately 35,600 canola contracts were traded as of 11:29 a.m. EDT, with prices in Canadian dollars per metric ton:

 
           Price      Change 
Nov       617.00     up 0.10 
Jan       628.40   unchanged 
Mar       639.00     dn 0.20 
May       648.90     dn 0.10

Source: Commodity News Service Canada, news@marketsfarm.com

data.title: Mexican Peso Weakens to 2-Week Lows
data.description:
The Mexican peso weakened to around 18.75 per USD in early September, marking a two-week low as a firmer US dollar combined with softer domestic flows and a Banxico policy path that looks increasingly gradual.

Remittances, a key source of dollar supply, fell 4.7% in July from a year earlier, extending a four-month streak of declines and removing a structural tailwind that had supported the peso earlier in 2025.

At the same time unemployment edged up to 2.8% in July from 2.7% in June, a modest softening that cools consumption and reinforces the case for further easing.

The central bank has started a measured easing cycle, cutting 25bp to 7.75% and signalling a slow, data dependent glide path, which reduces interest rate carry trade appeal.

Meanwhile the greenback has been holding near late August highs, adding further pressure on the Mexican currency.

data.title: FDA Plans Real-Time Release of Complete Response Letters
data.description:
By Colin Kellaher

The U.S. Food and Drug Administration said it will begin publishing rejection letters sent to drugmakers in real time as part of the agency's push for increased transparency.

The FDA on Thursday said it has published 89 decision letters, known as complete response letters, that it has issued since 2024 in response to applications for approval of drugs or biological products, and that it plans to release future letters promptly after they are issued to sponsors.

The FDA said all letters will be redacted to remove confidential commercial information, trade secrets and personal private information, but that they will contain company names.

The FDA issues complete response letters when it determines that it can't approve an application in its current form, mostly due to safety and efficacy concerns, manufacturing deficiencies or bioequivalence issues.

The agency in July published more than 200 letters issued between 2020 and 2024 and pledged to make more available in an effort to give the public more insight into its decision-making process and the most common deficiencies it cites.

The FDA at the time said companies often misrepresent the rationale behind the agency's decisions, and that lessons learned from non-approvals aren't shared within the industry, leading companies to repeatedly make similar mistakes.

Write to Colin Kellaher at colin.kellaher@wsj.com

Dutch auctions are also called uniform-price auctions.Successful bidders pay only the price of the lowest acceptedbid, rather than the actual price they bid as in amultiple-price auction.

data.title: Western Digital on Track for Record High Close — Data Talk
data.description:
Western Digital Corporation (WDC) is currently at $90.02, up $4.02 or 4.67%

  • Would be new all-time high (Based on available data back to Jan. 29, 1973)
  • Would be the first record closing high since Dec. 22, 2014, when it closed at $86.35
  • Currently up eight of the past nine days
  • Currently up three consecutive days; up 12.05% over this period
  • Best three day stretch since the three days ending July 31, 2025, when it rose 14.07%
  • Up 99.79% year-to-date
  • Up 90.28% from 52 weeks ago (Sept. 5, 2024), when it closed at $47.31
  • Would be a new 52-week closing high
  • Up 194.76% from its 52-week closing low of $30.54 on April 4, 2025
  • Traded as high as $90.04; new all-time intraday high (Based on available data back to Jan. 29, 1973)
  • Up 4.7% at today's intraday high
  • Third best performer in the S&P 500 today

All data as of 11:30:08 AM ET

Source: Dow Jones Market Data, FactSet
